name: Lacasa Of Goshen, Inc.
description_short: Elkhart County nonprofit providing housing counseling, financial education, and community development since 1970. Offers homebuyer prep, debt counseling, and affordable rental properties.
description_long:
Lacasa of Goshen, Inc. was founded in 1969 by area church leaders to address housing needs for migrant workers and has evolved into Elkhart County's primary organization for individual, family, and community empowerment. Operating for over 50 years, Lacasa maintains deep roots in the faith community while serving thousands of residents annually across multiple service areas.

Lacasa operates a full-service Financial Empowerment Center offering financial education classes (Master Your Money, Master Your Debt, Master Homeownership), matched savings accounts, individual debt reduction counseling, and goal-setting guidance. They provide HUD-approved housing counseling and financial coaching in both English and Spanish. Additionally, Lacasa manages approximately 400 affordable rental units across Elkhart County and offers the Help-a-House program for low-income homeowners needing repair and weatherization work. Services address renters, prospective homebuyers, and existing homeowners seeking stability.

Lacasa distinguishes itself through its integrated approach combining housing solutions with financial education and community revitalization. Their substantial real estate portfolio (400 rental units) demonstrates serious commitment to affordable housing provision rather than counseling-only services. Bilingual service delivery and weatherization/repair assistance through Help-a-House extend impact beyond traditional financial counseling. The organization's longevity and community partnerships create institutional credibility.

Lacasa is a legitimate, established nonprofit addressing genuine barriers to financial stability in Elkhart County. However, as a regional organization, they serve only Elkhart County residents. Their services are free or low-cost but require active participation in classes and counseling. Prospective clients should verify specific program eligibility and availability before assuming access to all advertised services.
pros: Bilingual financial coaching and housing counseling available in English and Spanish
Operates 400 affordable rental units providing direct access to safe, attractive housing
Help-a-House program provides free/subsidized home repairs and weatherization for low-income owners
Full-service Financial Empowerment Center with matched savings accounts and debt counseling
Over 50 years of community presence with deep church and neighborhood partnerships
Specific educational classes tailored to life stages (renting, buying, homeownership mastery)
Serves thousands of residents yearly with demonstrated impact across Elkhart County
cons: Service area limited to Elkhart County only—not accessible to out-of-region consumers
Website lacks specific information about program costs, eligibility requirements, and application timelines
No clear details on average counseling wait times or case manager assignment processes
Limited information about success rates or outcomes from financial education and debt counseling programs
Website does not specify income limits, credit score requirements, or other eligibility criteria
best_for: Elkhart County renters seeking affordable housing options with supportive services
First-time homebuyers in Elkhart County needing education, counseling, and matched savings programs
Low-income homeowners needing repair assistance and energy efficiency improvements
Spanish-speaking residents seeking bilingual financial counseling and housing guidance
diagnosis: Lacasa is ideal for Elkhart County residents seeking integrated housing solutions with financial education, particularly renters, prospective homebuyers, and homeowners needing repairs. The primary limitation is geographic: services are available only within Elkhart County, making this a regional rather than national resource. Free or low-cost services require active participation and commitment to counseling and education programs.
services: Housing counseling and financial coaching in English and Spanish
Master Your Money financial education classes
Master Your Debt education and individual debt counseling
Master Homeownership classes and homebuyer preparation
Help-a-House program for home repairs and weatherization
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
